2604.08377.title: SkillClaw: Let Skills Evolve Collectively with Agentic Evolver
2604.08377.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents such as OpenClaw rely on reusable skills to perform complex tasks, yet these skills remain largely static after deployment. As a result, similar workflows, tool usage patterns, and failure modes are repeatedly rediscovered across users, preventing the system from improving with experience. While interactions from different users provide complementary signals about when a skill works or fails, existing systems lack a mechanism to convert such heterogeneous experiences into reliable skill updates. To address these issues, we present SkillClaw, a framework for collective skill evolution in multi-user agent ecosystems, which treats cross-user and over-time interactions as the primary signal for improving skills. SkillClaw continuously aggregates trajectories generated during use and processes them with an autonomous evolver, which identifies recurring behavioral patterns and translates them into updates to the skill set by refining existing skills or extending them with new capabilities. The resulting skills are maintained in a shared repository and synchronized across users, allowing improvements discovered in one context to propagate system-wide while requiring no additional effort from users. By integrating multi-user experience into ongoing skill updates, SkillClaw enables cross-user knowledge transfer and cumulative capability improvement, and experiments on WildClawBench show that limited interaction and feedback, it significantly improves the performance of Qwen3-Max in real-world agent scenarios.
2604.08377.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理如 OpenClaw 依賴可重用的技能來執行複雜任務，但這些技能在部署後仍然大部分保持靜態。<lb/>因此，類似的工作流程、工具使用模式和失敗模式在用戶之間不斷被重新發現，阻礙了系統隨著經驗的增長而改進。<lb/>雖然來自不同用戶的互動提供了關於技能何時有效或失敗的互補信號，但現有系統缺乏將這些異質經驗轉化為可靠技能更新的機制。<lb/>為了解決這些問題，我們提出了 SkillClaw，一個在多用戶代理生態系統中進行集體技能演化的框架，將跨用戶和隨時間的互動視為改進技能的主要信號。<lb/>SkillClaw 持續聚合使用過程中生成的軌跡，並通過自主演化器處理這些軌跡，該演化器識別重複的行為模式，並將其轉化為對技能集的更新，通過精煉現有技能或用新能力擴展它們。<lb/>結果產生的技能被保存在共享庫中，並在用戶之間同步，允許在一個上下文中發現的改進在系統範圍內傳播，同時不需要用戶額外的努力。<lb/>通過將多用戶經驗整合到持續的技能更新中，SkillClaw 實現了跨用戶知識轉移和累積能力提升，並且在 WildClawBench 上的實驗顯示，有限的互動和反饋顯著改善了 Qwen3-Max 在現實世界代理場景中的性能。
2604.08377.author: Ziyu Ma et.al.
2604.08377.authors: Ziyu Ma, Shidong Yang, Yuxiang Ji, Xucong Wang, Yong Wang, Yiming Hu, Tongwen Huang, Xiangxiang Chu
